abstract: Removes the specified ACL entry from the access instance that contains it.
overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

This method fails if you attempt to remove the owner entry because an access instance must have exactly one such ACL at all times. If you need to change ownership settings, modify the existing owner entry rather than replacing it. In particular, use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessCopyMatchingACLList(_:_:)] method with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecACLAuthorizationChangeACL] authorization to find the existing entry, and the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLSetContents(_:_:_:_:)] method to change it as needed.

# SecACLRemove(_:)

**Function**

Removes the specified ACL entry from the access instance that contains it.

## Declaration

```swift
func SecACLRemove(_ aclRef: SecACL) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **aclRef**: An ACL entry to remove.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

This method fails if you attempt to remove the owner entry because an access instance must have exactly one such ACL at all times. If you need to change ownership settings, modify the existing owner entry rather than replacing it. In particular, use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessCopyMatchingACLList(_:_:)] method with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecACLAuthorizationChangeACL] authorization to find the existing entry, and the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLSetContents(_:_:_:_:)] method to change it as needed.
